---
# The title MUST start with "FIMS Weekly - "
title: "FIMS Weekly - April 27–May 01, 2026"
date: "April 27, 2026"
# Change the following categories accordingly, but you MUST keep fims-weekly
categories:
  - fims-weekly
three-big-things:
  bt1: "There are several Pull Requests that have been in the queue for more than a week that need reviewed and brought into dev. We are still thinking about changing our workflow to move away from having a dev branch but more investigation is needed."
  bt2: "Adrianne is thinking about how to model variability in length within the new growth module. Feel free to reach out to her if you know of any published papers or have opinions."
  bt3: "Matthew is continuing to work on translational units and getting FIMS to operate more like the RTMB package while compiling. The current road block is with the use of roxygen2 some additional files are added that can cause headaches."
# Announcements go below because of formatting that quarto doesnt transfer over from the yml
# If there is no code club or seaside chat, just delete those lines and the template will handle it for you
upcoming-events:
# If you have any additional events, uncomment and fill out the additional events section below.
# If there is no actual photo you can delete include-photo, path, and alt-text 
# and the template will handle it for you. 
# If there is code instead, please uncomment the section below named photo-code 
# but still include the text portion under photo.
photo:
  text: "In the priorities presentation during the CIE Review, I presented the following image, which I found on a blog post titled [Stop building what your customers ask for](https://dev.to/jon_at_backboardio/stop-building-what-your-customers-ask-for-3d16?context=digest). In short, the blog post reminded me how it is not only our job to produce a product that is of use to fisheries managers but it is also our job to interpret what stakeholders see as a problem and imagine a solution potentially outside of a workflow that currently exists. "
  include-photo: true
  path: "https://media2.dev.to/dynamic/image/width=1000,height=420,fit=cover,gravity=auto,format=auto/https%3A%2F%2Fdev-to-uploads.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fuploads%2Farticles%2F2fytab0wih4bn8ib230a.png"
  alt-text: "A cartoon illustrating to situations where developers blindly developed what the stakeholders asked for and ended up getting things wrong."
---

{{< include "fims-weekly-template.qmd" >}}

::: {#announcements}
- CIE Review is complete! Thank you to Andrea, Bai, Ian, and Nathan for giving presentations. Thank you to Aaron Berger for being such a great Chair. Thank you to everyone who took notes, they are extremely comprehensive and have already been used by several individuals to summarize the key points of the review. I really appreciate all of the help making this review successful. We received lots of good, constructive feedback and I am looking forward to working on implementing some of the suggested changes.
::: 

::: {#additional-events}
:::

<!-- ::: {#photo-code}
::: -->

```{=html}
<script src="https://giscus.app/client.js"
        data-repo="noaa-fims/FIMS"
        data-repo-id="R_kgDOGvEn5A"
        data-category="Blog discussions"
        data-category-id="DIC_kwDOGvEn5M4C-hls"
        data-mapping="title"
        data-strict="1"
        data-reactions-enabled="1"
        data-emit-metadata="0"
        data-input-position="bottom"
        data-theme="preferred_color_scheme"
        data-lang="en"
        crossorigin="anonymous"
        async>
</script>
```
